hero.sub: Let Daddy guide you through every step of the build. Daddy answers everything.

demo.ariaLabel: Demo conversation with Daddy Sr.PC about GPU compatibility.
demo.question: Does an RTX 4070 fit in a mini-ITX case?
demo.answer: Depends on the length. Most RTX 4070s measure 240–300mm. For mini-ITX, check the "max GPU length" spec of your case. Which one do you have? I'll tell you if it fits.

features.heading: Four domains, one agent that connects them all.

compat.title: Component compatibility
compat.body: Check socket, RAM, watts and cooling. Daddy analyzes your combo and tells you if it works.

build.title: Step-by-step assembly guides
build.body: From the socket to the thermal paste. Clear steps for your first build or an upgrade.

tech.title: In-depth technical details
tech.body: TDP, L3 cache, speeds, architectures. Everything you need to know about each chip.

best.title: Best practices
best.body: Cooling, cable management, ventilation and maintenance. Take care of your setup the way Daddy teaches.

howItWorks.heading: From the workshop to the answer, in four steps.

step1.title: Ask anything you want
step1.body: No filters, no taboos. Beginner technique or overclock debate — Daddy is ready.

step2.title: The agent replies with context
step2.body: No templates. Precise explanations, technical data and safety warnings where needed.

step3.title: You have 15 free queries
step3.body: Try it without signing up. The counter stays visible and drops with each question.

step4.title: Sign up to keep going
step4.body: Free with 15 queries per day, or continue without limits by creating your account.

demo.heading: Your first contact with Daddy. No sign-up.
demo.sub: These 15 queries are real. The counter drops with each question — when it hits zero, we invite you to keep going.
demo.welcome: Hey! I'm Daddy. Ask me anything about your PC build: compatibility, assembly, whatever. You have 15 queries to try.
demo.placeholder: E.g.: Does AM5 use DDR4 or DDR5?
demo.bannerSoft: {{ count }} free queries left. Sign up to keep going without limits.
demo.bannerStrong: 1 query left. Create your account to continue.

gate.title: You asked 15 questions. Nice work.
gate.text: Want to keep going? Sign up for free:
gate.fine: No surprises. Most users stick with the free plan.

novato.r1: Great question. In plain terms: make sure the CPU socket matches the motherboard, the RAM type is correct (DDR4 or DDR5) and the PSU has enough wattage. Tell me your components and I'll check them one by one.
novato.r2: No worries, it's easier than it looks. The key is to take it step by step and never force anything when seating parts. Want me to walk you through it?

intermedio.r1: Depends on the combo. Check three things: socket (AM5/LGA1700), RAM version (AM5 is DDR5 only) and combined TDP vs your PSU. Send me the models and I'll confirm if there's a bottleneck.
intermedio.r2: For that case, check the PCIe slot and GPU length against the case clearance. Tell me the case model and the graphics card.

avanzado.r1: Getting into detail: with an X3D, prioritize stable RAM frequency (EXPO 6000 CL30 is the sweet spot on AM5) over aggressive latencies. The bottleneck is usually the infinity fabric, not the core. What kit are you looking at?
avanzado.r2: Thermally, rated TDP is misleading: look at the actual PPT under sustained load and size the cooler with margin. I can give you specific numbers if you tell me the chip.

closing.sub: No fear of burning anything. The workshop mentor is waiting for your next question.

step1.title: What's your name?

errors.passwordUppercase: Include at least one uppercase letter

register.successBody: Welcome to the workshop. Build with Daddy without limits.
